channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: What HIPAA Can Teach Carriers About AI Governance
item.description: Carriers should expect AI governance to follow a similar trajectory—and prepare before regulation forces the issue. I use it every day, and I see enormous potential for it to help carriers move faster, reduce repetitive work and give claims professionals more time to focus on what matters. But carriers will eventually be asked what happened to the information behind that output.

item.title: Insurity Adds Confianza Risk Intelligence
item.description: (Image source: Confianza homepage.) Insurity (Hartford, Conn.) has integrated Confianza (Fernandina, Fla.) risk intelligence into its SpatialKey platform. Insurity says the relationship could expand over time to include additional data enrichment and risk intelligence capabilities across the insurance lifecycle. “We’re giving customers access to more complete property insights directly within their underwriting workflows by integrating Confianza’s specialized risk intelligence into SpatialKey.

item.title: Carpe Launches Claims Intelligence Suite
item.description: (Image source: Carpe website.) Carpe (Santa Barbara, Calif.) has launched Carpe Claims, a unified claims intelligence suite for property/casualty insurers. The suite brings together Carpe’s claims intelligence products with three new offerings: Carpe Case Management, Carpe Vision and Carpe AdWatch. Carpe Case Management gives claims and SIU teams a single workspace for parties, vehicles, documents and open-web intelligence related to a claim.

item.title: Overlapping Disasters Challenge States Amid Trump Cuts
item.description: As climate change makes compound events more likely, experts warned that grappling with them is further complicated by the Trump administration’s cuts to disaster work. Across the US last week, different disasters — from Canadian wildfire smoke darkening the skies in the Midwest and Northeast to extreme heat along the East Coast to catastrophic flooding in Texas — disrupted daily life and put people’s health at risk. The American disaster complex has long been struggling to cope with the increasing number of costly crises, but recent cuts to the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) have made the situation worse, according to Jeffrey Schlegelmilch, Columbia University’s faculty director of the National Center for Disaster Preparedness.

item.title: Supply outstripping demand, but El Nino, Middle East headwinds could shift market: Guy Carpenter’s Tony Gallagher
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Sompo to acquire Brazilian insurer Fator Seguradora
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Novo Nordisk Sues Eli Lilly in US Over Obesity Drug Ads
item.description: Novo Nordisk A/S sued Eli Lilly & Co., accusing its rival of misleading advertising as competition in the U.S. Novo says that Lilly’s advertising campaigns rely on outdated weight-loss comparisons with lower doses of Novo’s own obesity shot Wegovy than what’s currently available, according to the complaint. Though Lilly’s Zepbound beat Wegovy in a head-to-head trial last year, Novo subsequently got approval for a higher dose of its drug than what was used in that study.

item.title: Judge Approves Anthropic’s $1.5B Settlement of Copyright Lawsuit
item.description: A federal judge in San Francisco on Monday signed off on artificial intelligence company Anthropic’s landmark $1.5 billion settlement of a class action lawsuit brought by a group of authors who accused it of misusing their books to train its AI chatbot Claude. “We reached this settlement in 2025, after the court’s landmark ruling that training AI on books is fair use under copyright law — which remains the law today,” Anthropic deputy general counsel Aparna Sridhar said in a statement. District Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin granted final approval of the settlement, the largest known settlement of a U.S.

item.title: Amgen Settles Shareholder Suit Claiming It Hid $10.7B Tax Bill
item.description: Amgen reached a $74 million settlement of a lawsuit accusing the biotechnology company of waiting too long to disclose that it might owe the Internal Revenue Service $10.7 billion for underreporting six years of taxes. Amgen denied wrongdoing, and on Tuesday said it still believed the claims lacked merit. The IRS accused Amgen of underpaying taxes between 2010 and 2015, mainly for improperly shifting profits to a Puerto Rico unit that made many of its drugs.

item.title: Rising Pro Boxer Killed While Riding Bicycle in Bizarre Texas Crash
item.description: A rising professional boxer who was riding her bicycle along a rural Texas road died after officials say a motorist drove by her, stopped and then reversed into her. Hannah Rapp, 26, was riding with another person Saturday morning when she was hit, the Brazos County Sheriff’s Office said in a news release. She moved to Texas in 2024 to take a job as a fire and life safety inspector at Texas A&M.

item.title: Red Sea War Insurance Costs Rise After Houthi Blockade
item.description: The insurance cost of shipping goods through the Red Sea rose on Monday after Yemen’s Iran-aligned Houthis said they were imposing a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, escalating risks for merchant shipping, insurance industry sources said. Indicative war risk premiums rose to around 0.75% of the value of a ship, from around 0.3% on Friday before the Houthi announcement, according to the sources, who declined to be identified due to the sensitivity of the matter. Even a small change will mean hundreds of thousands of dollars in extra costs for a seven-day voyage.

item.title: Deepest Drilling Study Ever Provides Insight into Deadly Japan 2011 Tsunami
item.description: By drilling deeper into the seafloor than ever before, researchers discovered a thin, slippery layer of ancient clay that allowed the massive rupture to race all the way to the ocean floor, triggering enormous seafloor movement and a powerful tsunami, according to new research by Northern Arizona University. Scientists have uncovered a hidden weakness beneath the Pacific Ocean that helps explain why Japan’s catastrophic 2011 earthquake and tsunami became so devastating. The 2011 Japan earthquake rupture reached only about 15 miles below the seafloor, allowing the fault to break much closer to the ocean bottom.

item.title: Howden names Elizabeth Antoine as partner for aviation in Australia
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Capital efficiency, standardisation, securitization can reduce costs, increase relevance: Bain & Co
item.description: In addition, standardisation is seen as one way to drive greater uptake of insurance securitization, which Bain & Co’s consultants see as a way to drive a lower-cost of capital through the insurance and reinsurance industry, to enable market participants to do more with greater capital efficiency. In a new report, specifically focused on global insurance and reinsurance market participants and opportunities, Bain & Co’s consultants explain that despite the industry’s strong 2025, this “is not a reliable indicator of its long-term health.” Adding that, “Structural challenges around affordability and access, limited economic payoffs from technology investment, and competition across the insurance value chain remain unresolved and could limit future growth.” Lowering the cost of risk is seen as key and within this reducing the cost-of-capital and enhancing its efficiency. In a wide-ranging report, Bain & Company’s consultants explain that lower-cost and efficient capital is one of the keys to driving real and sustainable gains in a world where incumbents are being threatened.

item.title: McGill & Partners launches South Africa renewables insurance solution with GIB
item.description: Independent global specialty insurance and reinsurance broker McGill and Partners, and GIB, the largest independent South African insurance broker, have launched Protea Green, a new solution focused on the insurance of risks associated with renewable energy infrastructure in South Africa. Tom Sexton, Head of Renewables, Power and Energy at McGill and Partners, commented, “This solution represents a timely and strategic response to the evolving needs of South Africa’s renewable energy sector. “With the differentiated, data-driven and technology-enabled approach that McGill and Partners always brings, we are well-positioned to become a leading provider of insurance solutions for renewable energy in South Africa and across the continent.”

item.title: Bamboo Insurance adds California home insurance capacity through New MS Transverse partnership
item.description: Bamboo Insurance, a property insurance provider specialising in coverage solutions for challenging markets, has announced a new partnership with MS Transverse Insurance Company to increase its admitted homeowners and dwelling fire insurance capacity across California. “Our partnership with MS Transverse represents a meaningful step forward in how we approach both affordability and capacity in challenging markets,” commented John Chu, CEO of Bamboo Insurance. The collaboration provides around $150 million in additional admitted capacity and expands the range of options available through Bamboo Insurance’s existing product portfolio.

item.title: AXA accelerates Microsoft 365 Copilot rollout to employees worldwide as part of AI strategy
item.description: AXA, the France-headquartered multinational insurance and asset management group, has announced plans to introduce Microsoft 365 Copilot to employees worldwide, describing the move as the latest stage of its workplace AI strategy and a significant development in its wider programme to deploy artificial intelligence across the organisation. The addition of Microsoft 365 Copilot is expected to help employees provide a more personalised service in a secure environment while responding more effectively to the changing needs of both individual and commercial customers. The company said the rollout builds on the introduction of Secure GPT, its internal AI platform launched three years ago to provide employees and business applications with secure access to large language models (LLMs).

item.title: Former Arch Execs Grandisson, Gansberg Named Vantage Chair, CEO; Best’s Comments
item.description: Grandisson and Gansberg bring decades of specialty insurance leadership to Vantage, having spent much of their careers together at Arch Capital Group, which they helped build into one of the world’s most respected and profitable specialty insurers and reinsurers, according to Howard Hughes in a statement issued on July 16 . Howard Hughes Holdings Inc., the new parent company of Vantage Group Holdings Ltd., announced that Marc Grandisson has been named executive chairman of Vantage, effective immediately. Gansberg, who also joined Arch in 2001, led the company’s Global Mortgage Group as CEO from 2019 to 2024 and built it into a market leader, before being named president of Arch Capital Group with accountability for its Global Insurance Group.

item.title: People Moves: Liberty Mutual Re Names Spurgeon as Global Head of Cyber Reinsurance; Tokio Marine HCC Promotes Dagdelen as Head of Cyber – International
item.description: This edition of International People Moves details cyber appointments at Liberty Mutual Reinsurance and Tokio Marine. Liberty Mutual Re Names Spurgeon as Global Head of Cyber Reinsurance Liberty Mutual Reinsurance Group (LM Re), part of Liberty Mutual Insurance Group, has appointed Tom Spurgeon as global head of Cyber Reinsurance, reporting to Susan Barber , head of Financial Risks, Reinsurance. Spurgeon has been with Liberty Mutual Reinsurance for nine years and is a prominent figure in the reinsurance market, having recently been recognized for his leadership in the International Underwriting Association of London Cyber Reinsurance Committee.

item.title: Texas Heat Wave Collides With Tropical Storm Bertha
item.description: Texas and Louisiana are facing a dangerous combination of stormy weather and extreme temperatures as Tropical Storm Bertha sweeps across the Gulf Coast during a heat wave. Bertha is expected to bring a life-threatening storm surge and as much as 6 inches (15 centimeters) of rain, while temperatures climb well above 100F (38C). Added humidity from the storm will push heat index values as high as 112F in parts of the region, according to the National Weather Service.

item.title: Liberty Mutual Re appoints Tom Spurgeon as Global Head of Cyber Reinsurance
item.description: Liberty Mutual Reinsurance Group (LM Re) has appointed Tom Spurgeon as Global Head of Cyber Reinsurance , reinforcing its focus on expanding its global cyber reinsurance business. Leading global cyber portfolio growth Based in  London , Spurgeon will report to  Susan Barber , Head of Financial Risks, Reinsurance at LM Re. Internal promotion Spurgeon has spent the past  nine years  with Liberty Mutual Reinsurance Group and has played an active role in the wider cyber reinsurance market.

item.title: Blue Marble and MAPFRE partner to expand parametric climate insurance
item.description: Blue Marble Microinsurance has partnered with MAPFRE to expand access to parametric insurance solutions in markets with significant protection gaps. The collaboration brings together Blue Marble’s expertise in product design and risk modelling with MAPFRE’s local market knowledge and distribution capabilities to develop climate insurance products for underserved communities and businesses. Strengthening financial resilience By combining Blue Marble’s product development and modelling capabilities with MAPFRE’s local distribution network, the companies aim to broaden the availability of climate insurance while helping vulnerable communities and businesses strengthen their financial resilience.

item.title: Resting Heart Rate Reshapes Underwriting
item.description: Resting heart rate emerges as a powerful, underused mortality predictor that may outperform traditional underwriting metrics like cholesterol and BMI. However, recent RGA research challenges that assumption by highlighting the value of a familiar, easily measured metric: resting heart rate. Resting heart rate (RHR) – the number of heartbeats per minute when an individual is at rest – provides a real-time snapshot of cardiovascular efficiency and overall physiological health.

item.title: Venerable and Guardian reach agreement to transition assets to Venerable’s investment advisory platform
item.description: The post Venerable and Guardian reach agreement to transition assets to Venerable’s investment advisory platform appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Venerable Holdings announced that it has reached an agreement with The Guardian Life Insurance Company of America to transition approximately $4.5 billion in separate account assets currently held in Guardian’s variable products trust into mutual funds advised by SunAmerica Asset Management, a Venerable subsidiary. “This agreement reflects the strength and scale of our investment advisory platform and our ability to deliver a seamless outcome for policyholders and partners alike.

item.title: Atome partners with Chubb
item.description: Buy now, pay later platform Atome has launched the Atome Card Shield in partnership with Chubb Philippines. Chubb first partnered with Atome in 2024 to co-create a range of insurance products for customers in Singapore, Malaysia, the Philippines and Indonesia. Purchases made from fraudulent websites or fake sellers are also covered, as well as any unauthorized transactions made with the Atome Card.

item.title: Estrella Insurance family opens Miami burger restaurant
item.description: The family behind Estrella Insurance has opened Star Burger , a farm-to-table smashburger restaurant in Miami. Star Burger is a separate venture of the Estrella family, which founded the insurance business. Located at 4901 SW 8th Street, the 2,750-square-foot restaurant serves burgers made with grass-fed, grass-finished beef raised at the Estrella family’s ranch in Mt.

item.title: FDA Walks Back Positive Lab Test in Lettuce Cyclospora Outbreak
item.description: Taylor Farms said the “FDA has not identified a single positive product test result for cyclospora.” Health officials are using other methods to trace the outbreak back to Taylor Farms produce, given the short shelf-life of lettuce and the specific challenges of testing for cyclospora. The Food and Drug Administration on Sunday withdrew an earlier positive test that had tied Taylor Farms’ lettuce to the cyclospora outbreak, even as health officials said the supplier remains a focus of their investigation. The FDA had said Saturday that it had found a positive test linking cyclospora to Taylor Farms’ lettuce that wasn’t part of the current recall, suggesting it could widen.

item.title: Raised on Breaches, Digital Natives Think Differently About Cyber Risk
item.description: The spring 2026 cyber incident affecting the Canvas Learning Management System couldn’t have occurred at a worse time. school year approached, students and educators suddenly couldn’t access the platform central to their class operations. The disruption was a major story, but what happened next may be more important for insurers preparing to protect the next generation of policyholders.

item.title: Q2 Net Income at Travelers Soars 46% on Less Catastrophes, Favorable Reserves
item.description: Second-quarter 2026 net income at Travelers increased 46% to about $2.2 billion on lower catastrophe losses and favorable prior year reserve development. Related: Travelers Builds Insurance-Specific LLM Total underwriting income for Q2 was up $716 million to about $1.7 billion. Favorable reserve development on business insurance was $319 million in Q2 versus $79 million during the same time last year thanks to better than expected loss experience in workers’ compensation over multiple years and in commercial property for recent years, Travelers said.

item.title: Pinnacle Underwriting taps James Kyte as casualty, financial lines head for ANZ
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Dell Technologies Capital: How To Build A Deep-Tech Startup For A Market That Isn’t Ready Yet And Why AI Won’t Kill SaaS
item.description: Daniel Docter , managing director at Dell Technologies Capital , began his career as a technologist. His technical roots are reflective of Palo Alto, California-based Dell Technologies Capital’s broader team. Since its 2012 inception, Dell Technologies Capital has invested $1.8 billion across the enterprise stack and saw six high-profile exits at the end of 2025 alone.

item.title: Closing The Series A Gap Is The Next Great Opportunity For Black Founders In The AI Era
item.description: It’s that many Black founders raise partial seed rounds that leave them without enough operating flexibility to achieve the milestones required for institutional Series A financing. In 2026, conversations about Black founders and venture capital have focused on access to funding . For Black founders, who continue to receive a disproportionately small share of venture capital, the inability to secure fully funded seed rounds has become one of the greatest barriers to building venture-scale companies.

item.title: Franklin Templeton lifts cat bond conviction to strongly overweight, stays neutral on other ILS
item.description: Franklin Templeton Investment Solutions, the hedge fund-focused arm of the global asset manager, has updated its stance on catastrophe bonds from ‘overweight’ to ‘strongly overweight’ for Q3 2026, citing the asset class’s attractiveness, while its stance on other ILS remains unchanged at ‘neutral’ The second quarter of 2026 saw catastrophe bond and related insurance-linked securities (ILS) issuance hit nearly $11.33 billion, as a total 48 transactions came to market, making it the most active quarter for catastrophe bonds in the market’s history. Meanwhile, Franklin Templeton’s neutral stance on other insurance-linked securities (ILS), which includes private collateralized reinsurance, retrocession, and industry loss warranties (ILWs), remains unchanged. At the same time, Franklin Templeton noted that cat bond spreads have moderated from post-2022–2023 dislocation levels but rebounded from the lows that were seen in late 2025.

item.title: Hannover Re vehicle Kaith Re issues $14.94m Seaside Re private cat bond
item.description: Class 3 Bermuda-based insurer and segregated accounts vehicle Kaith Re Ltd., the structure owned by global reinsurance company Hannover Re, issued the $14.94 million Seaside Re (Series 2026-61) private catastrophe bond, or cat bond lite, transaction. vehicle, $103 million through Seaside Re issues and $24.5 million through LI Re private cat bonds. Hannover Re’s Bermuda-domiciled transformer vehicle Kaith Re Ltd.

item.title: Pershing Square Holdings adds Twelve Securis board member Ranjani Kearsley as INED
item.description: Bill Ackman linked investment holding company Pershing Square Holdings has appointed current Twelve Securis board executive Ranjani Kearsley, who works at investment firm B-FLEXION, as an independent non-executive director, a move that brings additional insurance-linked securities (ILS) experience to the Vantage Group linked firm. Ranjani Kearsley has been appointed as an independent non-executive director of the Pershing Square Holdings Board, with effect from July 20th 2026. The firm had an investment stake in and partnership with specialist ILS investment manager Securis Investment Partners and when Twelve Capital acquired Securis, becoming Twelve Securis in the process in 2024 , B-FLEXION maintained its partnership with the combined and larger ILS investment manager, while Kearsley continued as a Board member of Twelve Securis.

item.title: Europe’s largest insurers deliver steady premium growth and improved solvency in 2025, MAPFRE reports
item.description: MAPFRE Economics, the research arm of Spain-based global insurance group MAPFRE, has reported in its latest assessment of the European insurance sector that the region’s 20 largest insurers continued to increase premium income during 2025, although growth slowed compared with the previous year. MAPFRE Economics also identifies Italy as the largest overseas destination for Life insurance business among Europe’s leading insurers, while highlighting Asia as an increasingly important region due to its expanding middle-class population and long-term growth prospects. MAPFRE Economics found that Europe’s largest insurance groups generated combined premiums of €922.8 billion in 2025, an increase of 4.57% over the previous year.

item.title: Guy Carpenter’s revenue slips 2% in Q2’26 amid softer reinsurance market, but Marsh delivers strong growth
item.description: Global reinsurance broker Guy Carpenter, a Marsh business, has reported second-quarter revenue of $664 million, down around 2% on both a GAAP and underlying basis, with a softer reinsurance market as the likely culprit for the decline. For the first half of the year, Guy Carpenter’s revenue remained flat on an underlying basis at $1.9 billion. Guy Carpenter sits within Marsh’s broader Risk & Insurance Services segment alongside Marsh Risk.

item.title: Property renewal rates continue to soften at accelerated pace in Q2’26: CRC Group
item.description: CRC Group, an independent wholesale specialty insurance distributor, has released its Property REDY Index for Q2 2026, revealing that property renewal rates continued to soften at an accelerated pace. During the first half of 2026, average renewal rates declined by between 8.8% and 13.3% month over month, a significant increase from the monthly declines of 1.5% to 5.5% recorded during the same period in 2025. CRC Group noted that ample reinsurance capacity continues to support favourable market conditions.

item.title: Rising Professional Boxer Killed in Texas Bicycle Incident
item.description: A rising professional boxer who was riding her bicycle along a rural Texas road died after officials say a motorist drove by her, stopped and then reversed into her. At the same time, Rapp was training to be a professional boxer, achieving a record of 8-0-1. She moved to Texas in 2024 to take a job as a fire and life safety inspector at Texas A&M.

item.title: Ten Workers Die After India Hydro Project Tunnel Collapses
item.description: At least 10 workers died and more maybe trapped after an under-construction tunnel at a hydroelectric project collapsed in India’s northeastern state of Sikkim. The tunnel at state-run NHPC Ltd.’s 500-megawatt Teesta-VI project caved in around midday on Monday after a sudden burst of methane gas from the rocks triggered an explosion, the company said said in a stock exchange filing. The blast trapped 25 workers and officials in the tunnel located in Namchi district, it added.

item.title: WR Berkley Reports Q2 Net Income Increase of 12.7%
item.description: Operating income for the second quarter was $497.1 million, up from $420.5 million during the same time in 2025. Berkley’s insurance segment combined ratio for the period was 91.4 from 92.1 in Q2 2025.The reinsurance and monoline excess segment saw a decrease to 79.3 compared with 87.4. Underwriting income grew 21.8% to $317.5 million pretax, compared with Q2 2025.

item.title: Utica National appoints Abel Travis as Chief Operating Officer
item.description: Utica National Insurance Group has appointed Abel Travis as its new Chief Operating Officer , effective July 27, 2026 , as the insurer continues to strengthen its executive leadership team. Executive comments Kristen Martin, President and Chief Executive Officer of Utica National, said Travis brings significant operational and leadership experience to the company. Extensive insurance leadership experience Travis joins Utica National from  Brotherhood Mutual Insurance Company , where he served as  Senior Vice President of Affiliate Operations and Companies , leading a portfolio of businesses and transformation initiatives.

item.title: Marshmallow adopts Adclear AI platform to strengthen Consumer Duty compliance
item.description: Marshmallow has partnered with communications compliance platform Adclear to strengthen oversight of its financial promotions as UK financial services firms face increasing regulatory scrutiny under the Financial Conduct Authority’s Consumer Duty framework. With the FCA starting to scrutinise breaches of Consumer Duty, it’s never been more critical that brands adhere to the rules.” Expanding compliance technology Adclear’s platform is already used by several financial services firms, including  Lloyds Banking Group ,  PensionBee ,  IG Group ,  Freetrade ,  Yonder ,  Trade Nation ,  ActivTrades ,  Ocean Finance  and  Flagstone . The London-based insurtech will integrate Adclear’s AI-powered compliance technology into its existing approval process to support the review of marketing materials across its Motor Insurance , Motor Finance and Home Insurance products.

item.title: Marsh reports 6% revenue growth
item.description: Marsh Risk revenue increased 6% to $4.1 billion, including underlying growth of 4% in the US and Canada and 5% internationally. Marsh reported second-quarter 2026 revenue of $7.4 billion, up 6% from the prior-year period and 5% on an underlying basis. Mercer revenue rose 7% to $1.6 billion, and Marsh Management Consulting revenue increased 15% to $1 billion.

item.title: Reserv makes its claims AI stack available through AiDE
item.description: Reserv , a digital TPA handling P&C claims other than workers’ compensation, is licensing the technology used by its 600-person claims operation to Apeiros Insurance Data Exchange (AiDE), a separate company that will offer the same AI models to carriers. Rather than providing a finished user interface, AiDE will allow carriers and their systems integration partners to build their own workflows around the models already used by Reserv Claims Analysis, whose claims operation generates usage data that supports pricing and continued model development. Reserv is the parent company of Reserv Claims Analysis and Reserv Technologies.

item.title: Reserv CEO: Carriers don’t need SaaS wrappers around LLMs
item.description: Reserv CEO CJ Przybyl sits down with Coverager to discuss AiDE , the company’s early relationship with Accelerant, the rise of new claims competitors, and why carriers don’t need SaaS wrappers around LLMs. Carriers don’t need SaaS wrappers around LLM-based products; they can now build it better themselves. However, one of the main objectives when starting Reserv was to build the “clean room” to test claim technology quickly and share the learnings with the larger carriers that adjust claims in-house.

item.title: London Offices Without AC Become ‘Extremely’ Hard to Lease
item.description: Demand for London offices without AC was already lower than for buildings that had it as of 2024, according to property consultancy Knight Frank. Offices with no AC “will prove extremely challenging to let” as London temperatures continue to rise, says Dominic Pozzoni, head of national offices at Colliers. Related: Scientists Affirm Climate Change Fuels Extreme Weather There’s no recent public data on how widely air conditioning has been adopted across London’s offices.

item.title: Driving Above The Speed Limit Costs You — And Doesn’t Actually Save Much Time
item.description: They found that if drivers of light-duty, conventional internal combustion engine vehicles actually drove at posted speed limits, it could save an average of $22 million, based on fuel costs at the time, 6.7 million gallons of fuel and 57,000 metric tonnes of carbon dioxide every day. More than 43% of the studied trips included at least one instance of speeding, and drivers spent nearly 12% of their driving time going faster than the speed limit. If your objective is to get to your destination safely and to save fuel, then you might drive slower than the speed limit,” said William Northrop, mechanical engineering professor at the University of Minnesota and study co-author.

item.title: Lloyd’s coverholder Salt hires Chris Dent as marine manager
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Howden bolsters Japan natural resources team with Junichiro Ito as new head
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles
